incorrects.description: Student placed a wrong tile on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Skip-count by 7s: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35. How many times did you count?

incorrects.description: Student placed a wrong tile on second attempt

params.description: Clusters highlight one at a time while skip-counting: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35.

steps.text: Watch the clusters highlight as we skip-count by 7. First cluster: 7. Second: 14. Third: 21. Fourth: 28. Fifth: 35. Five groups of 7 equals what total?

incorrects.description: Student placed a wrong tile on third attempt. System models the correct answer.

params.description: All 5 clusters highlight sequentially while counting: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35.
Tile 35 moves into the product slot. Equation completes: 35 = 5 × 7.

steps.text: Let me show you. I skip-count by 7 five times: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35. Five groups of 7 is 35 total. The unknown is 35. That is the product, and it is the same value as 5 times 7.
